text.title: Debt Prevention: CI Gates and Pre-Commit Hooks
text.summary: Automated quality gates and pre-commit hooks that prevent debt accumulation without requiring manual discipline.

section.title: CI Quality Gates
section.reference: GitHub Actions workflow: complexity, coverage, duplication, dep audit, TODO count

rule.statement: Gate on cyclomatic complexity (radon cc, threshold 10), test coverage (80% minimum), duplicate code (pylint), vulnerable dependencies (safety/npm audit), and TODO count (<= 50 unlinked markers).
rule.rationale: Each gate targets a distinct debt type. Combining them in CI means every PR is evaluated before merge; manual code review catches things automation misses, but automation catches things code review skips.

section.p: Tools: radon (Python complexity), lizard (multi-language), pytest --cov (coverage), pylint --enable=duplicate-code, safety (Python deps), npm audit / cargo audit.

rule.statement: Enforce TODO(PROJ-123): description format via pre-commit hook. Reject commits with unlinked TODO/FIXME/HACK/XXX/TECH_DEBT markers.
rule.rationale: Unlinked debt markers are invisible in project management tools. Ticket-linked markers create an audit trail, appear in sprint planning, and get closed when the ticket is resolved.

example.code:
# Good: linked marker # TODO(PROJ-123): Replace this with the new billing service once TD-045 is resolved
# Bad: unlinked marker (will be rejected by pre-commit hook) # TODO: fix this later # FIXME: this is a hack

section.title: Hotspot Finder
section.reference: Bash script: cross-reference git churn with lizard complexity
section.p: Run monthly or before planning a refactoring sprint. Output: top-20 files by (churn_count, complexity) — these are the highest-leverage targets.

antipattern.description: Pre-commit gates configured too strictly (threshold 5 complexity on legacy code).
antipattern.reason: Over-strict gates become bypassed with --no-verify, losing all enforcement. Set thresholds at current median + 10%, then tighten each quarter.
CI catches regressions after the fact; developers get slow feedback. Pre-commit gates catch at the moment of writing, when fixing is cheapest.
